publication.theme: Tanger Pays $60M for Ohio Town Center as Open-Air Retail Consolidation Accelerates

slides.headline: Tanger Pays $60M for Ohio Town Center as Open-Air Retail Consolidation Accelerates
slides.subhead: On May 28, 2026, Tanger closed on a $60 million acquisition of The Town Center at Levis Commons, a 300,000-square-foot mixed-use development in Perrysburg, Ohio. The seller: Hill...

slides.headline: The clean read
bullets: On May 28, 2026, Tanger closed on a $60 million acquisition of The Town Center at Levis...
The company has been repositioning its portfolio toward higher-quality open-air assets...
For Tanger, the deal adds a stabilized cash-flowing asset with a weighted average lease...

slides.headline: The numbers tell the story
bullets: The price works out to $200 per square foot for a center that includes 60-plus retailers, a...
Tanger is a publicly traded REIT with a market cap of approximately $3.5 billion.

slides.headline: This is a capital stack story
slides.subhead: The firm is not a public REIT and faces no quarterly earnings pressure, but the decision to sell now suggests a view that retail cap rates have compressed to near-cycle lows.
bullets: The deal also signals that debt markets are open for high-quality retail acquisitions.
What this means for the broader market: consolidation is accelerating.
